commit_scope: Extend parsed BML BodySource execution with real thesis method-body expression shapes: member-add return values and implicit self method calls with one argument.

local_validation.notes: The first run without core.fk failed uniformly across sibling kernels with unbound function cell. That was a prelude gap: BML scanner tokens are compiler cells. With core.fk in the proof prelude, existing and new thesis proofs pass across Go, Rust, and TypeScript kernels.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A .bml method body parsed from the thesis companion corpus can now execute return m_strName.HashCode + m_hItem.HashCode; as a native return value and HandleArgs(hArgs); as an implicit self dispatch through method_invoke.
test_flows: python3 scripts/audit_bml_bmf_kernel_natives.py before commit to prove no new BML/BMF host-native semantics were introduced.

phase_gate.reason: This adds two real source-originated method body expression rungs. Full thesis parity still requires receiver calls, conditionals, loops, casts, comparisons, exceptions, and full BMF rule execution from the known-working companion code.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/grammars/bml.fk defines return member-add evaluation and implicit call1 execution in the BML language layer.
form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-rule-hashcode-return-proof.fk proves the original Rule.HashCode body returns through direct execution and method_invoke.
form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-bmf-main-call-proof.fk proves the original BMF.Main body dispatches HandleArgs(hArgs) through method_invoke.
